findings.title: Math.max(1, NaN) propagates NaN as maxAttempts — stale tasks never recovered
findings.detail: Previously the TaskStore constructor used Math.max(1, Number(env)) without a NaN guard, causing maxAttempts to be NaN when IFLEET_MAX_ATTEMPTS was set to a non-numeric value. This broke recoverStale() which uses maxAttempts in SQL comparisons. Fixed in current code: Number.isFinite() guard with default of 5.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — Number.isFinite guard present with default fallback to 5.

findings.title: buildCodexArgs passes brief directly as positional arg — brief starting with '-' treated as flag
findings.detail: Previously buildCodexArgs in codex.ts might not have had the '--' end-of-options separator before the brief positional argument, allowing a brief starting with '-' to be misinterpreted as a CLI flag. Fixed in current code: '--' separator is explicitly present in both the resume and fresh-start branches before opts.brief.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — '--' end-of-options separator is present in buildCodexArgs before opts.brief in both code paths.

findings.title: sprint_goal missing required fields return 500 instead of 4xx
findings.detail: In server.ts, onSprintGoal validates that channelId, userId, and userLabel are present and throws an Error when missing. control-plane.ts handleRequest catches all onSprintGoal exceptions as 500 Internal Server Error. Missing required fields are a client error (400 Bad Request), not a server error, so callers cannot distinguish between misconfiguration and a server bug.
findings.fix_sketch: Export a BadRequestError class from control-plane.ts. In server.ts onSprintGoal, throw BadRequestError for missing fields. In handleRequest, catch BadRequestError specifically and respond with 400 instead of 500.

findings.title: Concurrent cost-log appends can interleave and corrupt costs.json lines
findings.detail: appendCostRecord in src/utils/costs.ts uses fs.appendFile which is not atomic at the application level. Multiple concurrent pipeline phases (architect, editor, reviewer) can call appendCostRecord simultaneously from different tasks, causing JSON line interleaving in costs.json. readCostLog silently skips unparseable lines, so interleaved writes result in silent data loss.
findings.fix_sketch: Use appendFileSync (synchronous, process-level atomic on most OS) or add a per-process write queue/mutex. Since this is always within one Node process, a simple async queue (one pending write at a time) eliminates the race.

findings.title: Git auth token exposed via process args visible to local ps
findings.detail: GitRepoManager.authPrefix() constructs git -c http.extraheader=AUTHORIZATION: bearer TOKEN as a CLI argument. This is visible to any user who can read /proc/<pid>/cmdline or run 'ps aux' on the host. The comment acknowledges this as acceptable on the dedicated VPS, but it remains a risk if the VPS is ever shared or monitored.
findings.fix_sketch: Use GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 / GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0 / GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0 environment variables (git >=2.32) to pass the extraheader config without it appearing in argv. These are visible in /proc/<pid>/environ but require explicit read access, not just ps.

findings.title: DockerSandboxRunner mounts worktree as root with no read-only flag
findings.detail: buildDockerArgs in src/agents/verifier/sandbox.ts passes '--user root' to docker run, meaning all pnpm/build/test commands inside the container run as root. If a malicious package.json script or test escape is triggered, the container process has root privileges over the mounted /work volume.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove '--user root' and update pnpm store path from /root/.pnpm-store to /pnpm-store (a non-root-owned mount). Alternatively, use '--user 1000:1000' and pre-create the /pnpm-store directory with matching permissions in the Docker image build.

findings.title: TaskStore insert never persists the attempts column it relies on
findings.detail: The INSERT in TaskStore.insert() does not include the attempts column. Because the column has DEFAULT 0, new inserts correctly start at 0. However any future backfill or restore path that passes a task with attempts > 0 would silently ignore the value. The schema migration only adds the column for existing rows; new inserts should be explicit.
findings.fix_sketch: Already effectively fixed: the column DEFAULT 0 handles new inserts correctly and recoverStale() is the only path that increments attempts. No restore path exists today. Closing as fixed — if a backfill path is added later it should explicitly include the column.

findings.title: runner.ts logCosts uses task.id as sprintId fallback, corrupting sprint-level cost aggregation
findings.detail: In src/pipeline/runner.ts logCosts(), 'const sprintId = input.sprintId ?? input.task.id' silently uses the taskId as the sprintId when the caller omits sprintId. Since sprint IDs and task IDs have different namespaces and formats, the costs.json cost aggregation groups costs under fake sprint IDs that no real sprint references, making sprint-level cost tracking unreliable.
findings.fix_sketch: Emit a console.warn when sprintId is undefined and use a dedicated sentinel prefix like 'orphan-sprint:' + task.id to make misuse detectable. Or skip logging entirely when sprintId is absent.

findings.title: defaultHaikuCall passes full prompt (including user brief) as argv — visible in ps aux
findings.detail: In src/classifier/auto-router.ts, defaultHaikuCall calls execFile('claude', ['--print', prompt, '--model', model]). The prompt includes the full user task brief, which is visible to any process that can read /proc/<pid>/cmdline or run ps aux. This leaks user task content to any co-tenant on the VPS.
findings.fix_sketch: Write the prompt to a temp file, pass via stdin redirect using the claude CLI's stdin support, and delete the temp file after. Or switch to the Anthropic SDK API call (no subprocess, no argv exposure).

findings.title: issue-commenter.ts lastCommentId mutable closure races if comment() called before waitForApproval resolves
findings.detail: Previously lastCommentId was a mutable closure variable shared across comment() and waitForApproval() calls. A second comment() call mid-poll would overwrite lastCommentId, causing waitForApproval() to poll the wrong comment. Fixed: waitForApproval() now captures 'const commentId = lastCommentId' at call time, freezing the value for the poll loop.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — waitForApproval() captures commentId from lastCommentId at call start and uses that frozen value throughout the poll loop.

findings.title: queue/github.ts findLabelAddedAt paginates all issue events with no page count cap
findings.detail: findLabelAddedAt() calls octokit.paginate() with no page limit, fetching all issue events to find the latest 'labeled' event. An issue with thousands of events (e.g. a long-running discussion) triggers many API calls synchronously, exhausting the rate limit budget in the cooldown check path and blocking queue polling.
findings.fix_sketch: Use octokit.paginate.iterator() with a break condition to stop after finding the latest label event, or cap at MAX_PAGES (e.g. 20 pages = 2000 events). Since the function wants the LATEST event, scan pages in reverse or stop after a configurable max.

findings.title: DockerSandboxRunner falls back to in-worktree execution without requiring IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK
findings.detail: Previously DockerSandboxRunner would fall back to running pnpm scripts directly in the worktree when Docker was unreachable, without any production guard. This allowed worker-authored test scripts to execute directly with the daemon's process env and credentials. Fixed: production now requires IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK=1 to enable fallback.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — production (NODE_ENV=production) now errors when Docker is unreachable unless IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK=1 is set.

findings.title: workers/claude.ts passes full brief as -p argv — visible in ps aux for all pipeline phases
findings.detail: buildClaudeArgs() in packages/orchestrator-core/src/workers/claude.ts passes the full (possibly wrapped) brief as the value of the '-p' CLI flag. This includes architect plans, editor instructions, and the user task brief. The complete brief is visible in ps aux / /proc/<pid>/cmdline to any process on the VPS.
findings.fix_sketch: The claude CLI's '-p' flag is the only reliable prompt delivery mechanism (stdin was unreliable per existing comments). This is a known trade-off on a single-tenant VPS. Document as accepted risk or implement a temp-file approach if co-tenancy ever applies.

findings.title: awaitApproval second call overwrites first deferred — first caller promise leaks permanently
findings.detail: Previously a second awaitApproval() call for the same taskId would overwrite the pending Map entry without resolving the first caller's Promise, causing it to leak and never settle. Fixed: the displaced entry's resolve function is now called with 'cancel' before the new entry is registered, and the timer/abortSignal cleanup is performed.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — displaced.resolve('cancel') is called before pending.set() for the new entry, properly settling and cleaning up the first caller.

findings.title: auto-router.ts hashInput excludes repoRoot — cross-repo cache collision returns wrong routing decision
findings.detail: Previously hashInput() in src/classifier/auto-router.ts did not include repoRoot in the hash payload, meaning two tasks with the same title/body/labels from different repos shared the same cache key and returned the same (potentially wrong) routing decision. Fixed: repoRoot is now included in the JSON payload as field 'r'.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — hashInput() includes repoRoot as field 'r' in the JSON payload before hashing.

findings.title: audit-ritual.ts execSync interpolates indexPath into shell string — command injection on unsafe repo paths
findings.detail: Previously audit-ritual.ts may have used execSync with a shell-interpolated string containing indexPath, enabling command injection if indexPath contained shell metacharacters. Fixed: the code now uses execFileSync('npx', ['tsx', 'scripts/sync-audit-findings.ts', indexPath], ...) which passes indexPath as a separate argv element without shell interpretation.
findings.fix_sketch: Already fixed — uses execFileSync with array arguments, not shell string interpolation.

findings.title: classifyTask imported but never used in src/pipeline/factory.ts — causes lint CI failure
findings.detail: factory.ts imports 'classifyTask' from '../classifier/index.js' but only uses 'modelToTier' from the same import. The unused classifyTask import triggers eslint @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars as an error, causing pnpm lint to fail. This blocks CI and any automated lint gate.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove 'classifyTask' from the named import: change 'import { classifyTask, modelToTier }' to 'import { modelToTier }'.

findings.title: pipeline/reviewer.ts is a deprecated re-export shim with no enforced removal deadline
findings.detail: src/pipeline/reviewer.ts re-exports everything from diff-reviewer.ts with a comment saying 'Remove this file after the next release cuts.' No production code imports from this shim (all callers already use diff-reviewer.js directly). The shim adds unnecessary indirection and confusion about which module is canonical.
findings.fix_sketch: Delete src/pipeline/reviewer.ts. Verify no tests import from './reviewer.js' or '../reviewer.js' before deleting.

findings.title: auto-router in-memory cache never expires — stale routing decisions persist across sprint submissions
findings.detail: The in-process Map cache in src/classifier/auto-router.ts has no TTL or size limit. While the cache is cleared on daemon restart (the comment says 'One-process lifetime'), a long-running process accumulates stale entries as SPRINT.md and routing.json evolve. A task re-submitted after a routing config change still gets the old cached decision.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a TTL per entry (e.g. 30 min) or a max size with LRU eviction. Alternatively, expose the clearCache export (already present) and call it after routing.json/SPRINT.md is reloaded.

findings.title: contracts/hmac.ts verifyPayload has redundant length check after timingSafeEqual
findings.detail: verifyPayload() checks 'raw.length === expected.length' after timingSafeEqual(). This is intentional: when the provided signature had a different length than expected, the padded buffer (alloc(expected.length)) always produces a zeros-mismatch in timingSafeEqual, but the length check makes the intent explicit. It is a defensive guard, not truly redundant.
findings.fix_sketch: Already intentional — the post-timingSafeEqual length check prevents padding-match confusion. The comment explains the rationale. No change needed; marking as fixed (not a defect).

findings.title: server.ts deferringDiscordOut.postTaskCreated always returns empty threadId string without warning
findings.detail: deferringDiscordOut() in server.ts returns { threadId: '' } from postTaskCreated. Callers that check the returned threadId get an empty string with no indication that thread creation was deferred. This is by design (thread creation is deferred to the daemon), but a log message or explicit sentinel value would make the deferral visible.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a console.debug log line in postTaskCreated explaining the deferral, or return a distinct sentinel like { threadId: 'deferred' } so callers can distinguish 'not yet created' from 'unknown error'.

findings.title: audit-store.ts dbReadIndex returns null for both DB unreachable and zero-row cases without distinguishing them
findings.detail: dbReadIndex() returns null when the database is unreachable AND when the database has zero findings for the repo. The interaction-create.ts caller treats both cases identically (falls back to local file), but a zero-row DB result and a connection failure have different recovery actions: zero rows is a valid state, connection failure is a transient error that should be retried or alerted.
findings.fix_sketch: Return a discriminated union { ok: true; index: AuditIndex | null } | { ok: false; error: string } so callers can distinguish unreachable-DB from empty-results.

findings.title: audit-runner.ts markFindingsClosed batch path skips per-fingerprint dedup that appendToClosedJson uses
findings.detail: appendToClosedJson() checks 'cdata.closures.some(c => c.fingerprint === record.fingerprint)' before pushing a closure record, preventing duplicates. markFindingsClosed() uses 'cdata.closures.push(...records)' without the same dedup check. If a batch close is called twice for the same findings (e.g., audit-ritual reconcile retry), the closed.json grows with duplicate fingerprint entries.
findings.fix_sketch: In markFindingsClosed(), before pushing records, filter out any entry whose fingerprint already appears in cdata.closures, matching the dedup logic in appendToClosedJson.

findings.title: candidate-gen.ts unused _cfg parameter signal
findings.detail: generateCandidates() in src/agents/proposer/candidate-gen.ts has a parameter '_cfg: ProposerConfig' that is never read inside the function. The underscore prefix indicates intentional suppression, but it's unclear whether cfg was removed from the implementation or forgotten. If _cfg is genuinely not needed, removing it from the signature (and all callers) cleans up the API surface.
findings.fix_sketch: If _cfg is no longer needed, remove it from the function signature and update all callers. If it may be needed in a future iteration, add a comment explaining why it's retained.
